summary: Dan Shapiro announces he has joined Wharton's Generative AI Lab as a research fellow, working alongside Ethan and Lilach Mollick. He plans to share lessons learned from building generative AI tools at Glowforge and investigate unusual phenomena he's observed in LLMs. He emphasizes this is a side project alongside his continued role as Glowforge CEO. The post is brief and serves mainly as a teaser for upcoming content.
key_points: Shapiro has joined Wharton's Generative AI Lab as a research fellow while remaining Glowforge CEO
He plans to share practical lessons from building generative AI tools at Glowforge
He's observed 'strange and truly bizarre phenomena' in the world of LLMs that he wants to investigate
The post signals a return to more active blogging after a quiet period
money_quotes: I'm working with them to share some of the the lessons we've learned building generative AI tools at Glowforge so more people can put them to work.
I'm excited to investigate some of the strange and truly bizarre phenomena I've been seeing in the world of LLMs.
key_insight: Shapiro is bridging the gap between hands-on AI product development at Glowforge and academic research at Wharton, positioning himself to formalize and share observations about LLM behavior.
